questions.text: The primary valency of the central metal ion in [Co(NH₃)₆]Cl₃ is:
questions.explanation: Primary valency corresponds to the oxidation state of the metal. Here Co is in +3 state to balance 3 Cl⁻ ions.

questions.text: Which of the following ligands is an example of an ambidentate ligand?
questions.explanation: Ambidentate ligands like SCN⁻ can coordinate through two different atoms (S or N).

questions.text: The coordination number of Cr in [Cr(en)₃]³⁺ is:
questions.explanation: Ethane-1,2-diamine (en) is a didentate ligand. 3 ligands × 2 donor sites = 6.

questions.text: According to Crystal Field Theory, for an octahedral complex with d⁴ configuration, if Δo < P, the configuration is:
questions.explanation: If pairing energy (P) is greater than splitting (Δo), electrons will enter eg rather than pairing, leading to high spin.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of [Pt(NH₃)₂Cl(NO₂)] is:
questions.explanation: Ligands are named alphabetically: diammine, chloro, nitrito-N.

questions.text: Which type of isomerism is shown by [Co(NH₃)₅(SO₄)]Br and [Co(NH₃)₅Br]SO₄?
questions.explanation: These produce different ions (Br⁻ vs SO₄²⁻) in solution.

questions.text: Optical isomerism is most commonly shown by:
questions.explanation: Cis-isomers of octahedral complexes with didentate ligands lack a plane of symmetry.

questions.text: The shape of [Ni(CN)₄]²⁻ is:
questions.explanation: CN⁻ is a strong field ligand causing dsp² hybridization in Ni²⁺.

questions.text: A complex with Δo > P is called:
questions.explanation: When splitting is large, electrons pair up (low spin) using inner d-orbitals.

questions.text: Which of the following is a neutral ligand?
questions.explanation: Carbonyl (CO) is a neutral ligand; others are anionic.

questions.text: The magnetic moment of [NiCl₄]²⁻ is (Atomic no. Ni = 28):
questions.explanation: Ni²⁺ is d⁸. Cl⁻ is weak, so it is sp³ tetrahedral with 2 unpaired electrons. μ = √[2(2+2)] = √8 ≈ 2.82.

questions.text: EDTA⁴⁻ is a ________ ligand.
questions.explanation: EDTA has 4 oxygen and 2 nitrogen donor atoms.

questions.text: What is the EAN of Fe in [Fe(CN)₆]⁴⁻?
Linkage isomerism arises in complexes containing:
questions.explanation: It occurs when a ligand can bind to the metal through different donor atoms.

questions.text: The complex [Co(NH₃)₄Cl₂]Cl shows:
questions.explanation: It exists in cis and trans forms.

questions.text: In crystal field splitting in tetrahedral complexes:
questions.explanation: In tetrahedral complexes, t2 orbitals (dxy, dyz, dzx) are raised higher in energy than e orbitals.

questions.text: Which of the following is most stable?
questions.explanation: The presence of chelating ligands (like oxalate) increases stability (Chelate effect).

questions.text: According to Werner, secondary valency is:
questions.explanation: Secondary valency is non-ionizable and corresponds to the coordination number.

questions.text: The number of ions given by [Pt(NH₃)₄Cl₂]Cl₂ in water is:
questions.explanation: It dissociates into one [Pt(NH₃)₄Cl₂]²⁺ ion and two Cl⁻ ions.

questions.text: Fac-mer isomerism is associated with complexes of type:
questions.explanation: Facial (fac) and Meridional (mer) isomers occur in octahedral complexes with MA₃B₃ stoichiometry.

questions.text: Which ligand is used for estimation of hardness of water?
questions.explanation: EDTA forms stable complexes with Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ ions responsible for hardness.

questions.text: The spectrochemical series is based on:
questions.explanation: It arranges ligands in increasing order of their crystal field splitting energy (CFSE).

questions.text: Which ion is paramagnetic?
questions.explanation: Cu²⁺ is d⁹, having one unpaired electron. Others are d¹⁰ or have paired electrons.

questions.text: Synergic bonding is observed in:
questions.explanation: Synergic bonding involves σ-donation from ligand and π-back donation from metal to ligand anti-bonding orbitals.

questions.text: The correct order of field strength of ligands is:
questions.explanation: Halides are weak field; CN⁻ is a strong field ligand.

questions.text: A homoleptic complex is one which:
options: Contains only one type of ligand
questions.explanation: Example: [Co(NH₃)₆]³⁺. If it has different types of ligands, it is heteroleptic.

questions.text: Chlorophyll is a coordination compound of:
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll contains a magnesium porphyrin complex.

questions.text: Hemoglobin contains coordination compound of:
questions.explanation: Hemoglobin contains heme, which is an iron-porphyrin complex.

questions.text: Vitamin B₁₂ contains coordination compound of:
questions.explanation: Vitamin B₁₂ (Cyanocobalamin) contains cobalt.

questions.text: Wilkinson's catalyst is used in:
questions.explanation: [(Ph₃P)₃RhCl] is used for the selective hydrogenation of alkenes.

questions.text: In [Ni(CO)₄], the hybridization of Ni is:
questions.explanation: Ni is in 0 oxidation state (d⁸s²). CO is strong, causing pairing and d¹⁰ state. Hybridization is sp³.

questions.text: Cis-platin is used in the treatment of:
questions.explanation: Cis-[Pt(NH₃)₂Cl₂] is a common chemotherapy drug.

questions.text: Which of the following is an inner orbital complex?
questions.explanation: NH₃ is a strong field ligand for Co³⁺, using 3d orbitals for d²sp³ hybridization.

questions.text: The unit of crystal field splitting energy is:
questions.explanation: Energy can be expressed in various units including wave numbers (cm⁻¹).

questions.text: The coordination number of metal in [M(en)₂(C₂O₄)]Cl is:
questions.explanation: en (bidentate) × 2 + C₂O₄ (bidentate) × 1 = 4 + 2 = 6.

questions.text: Optical isomerism is NOT possible in:
questions.explanation: The trans isomer has a center of symmetry and is optically inactive (achiral).

questions.text: The ratio of Δt to Δo is approximately:
questions.explanation: Δt = 4/9 Δo. Splitting is much smaller in tetrahedral fields.

questions.text: Ligands are ________ in nature.
questions.explanation: Ligands donate a pair of electrons to the metal atom, acting as Lewis bases.

questions.text: The complex [Fe(CN)₆]³⁻ is:
questions.explanation: Fe³⁺ is d⁵. CN⁻ is strong, so configuration is t2g⁵ with one unpaired electron.

questions.text: In IUPAC naming, the name of anionic ligand Cl⁻ is:
questions.explanation: Anionic ligands end in '-o'.
